prs.title: Rename escape_char and unescape_char to private
prs.body:
## Description
I've recreated this PR on a fresh branch to fix the version conflicts and the "old version" issue from the previous attempt. 

### Changes:
*  Updated `escape_char` and `unescape_char` to `_escape_char` and `_unescape_char` to make them private.
*  Added the entry in `CHANGES.rst` under the correct `v7.0.0a2` header.
*  Verified that all internal calls are updated and tests pass.
* 
This PR replaces #1222 to provide a conflict-free integration.

prs.title: Fix #10899: Restore None hyperlink in py domain
prs.body:
## Description

Since Sphinx 4.4.0, the `None` singleton is no longer hyperlinked in type annotations.

This occurs because `None` is currently treated using the `class` role instead of the `obj` role during cross-reference generation.

However, `None` is not a type — it is a singleton object. Therefore, it should be referenced using the `:obj:` role to generate correct cross-references.

## Changes

- Override the `make_xref()` method in `PyField` and `PyTypedField`
- Detect when `rolename == "class"` and `target == "None"`
- Switch the role from `"class"` to `"obj"` to ensure proper linking
- Add test coverage to verify correct cross-referencing of `None`

## Related Issues

Closes #10899  
Closes collective/icalendar#1152

prs.title: Rename private helpers to underscore-prefixed
prs.body:
## Closes issue
- [x] Closes #1011
- [x] Supersedes #1125

## Description
Following up on the feedback from #1125, I've opened this new PR from a clean branch to fix the naming issues and get the tests passing properly. 

I've made sure to fix the "conceptual errors" mentioned last time—specifically correcting the double-underscore names and ensuring private functions are actually removed from `__all__` rather than added to it. I also reverted the class name changes to keep the public API stable.

There are some merge conflicts with the latest `main` branch, which I'll resolve using the GitHub editor as soon as this is open.

## Checklist
- [x] Fixed naming to use single `_` prefix (no more double/triple underscores).
- [x] Removed private helpers from `__all__` in `tools.py`, `parser_tools.py`, and `cli.py`.
- [x] Kept `CaselessDict`, `Contentline`, and `Contentlines` as public classes.
- [x] Updated all internal imports and call sites.
- [x] Verified that local tests are passing.

<!-- readthedocs-preview icalendar start -->
----
📚 Documentation preview 📚: https://icalendar--1221.org.readthedocs.build/

<!-- readthedocs-preview icalendar end -->

prs.title: feat: Add data_loader module for loading and validating dataset
prs.body:
### Description
This PR introduces the core data loading module for the mosquito taxon classifier. It ensures that the input features and labels are loaded correctly and validated before being passed to the model pipeline.

### Changes
1. **Data Loading:** Implemented `data_loader.py` to handle `.tsv` files.
2. **Validation:** Added logic to ensure feature and label counts are consistent.
3. **Documentation:** Added type hints and Google-style docstrings for better maintainability.
4. **Testing:** Included `tests/test_loader.py` to verify the loading logic works as expected.

### Testing Done
Verified that `features.tsv` and `labels.tsv` load without errors.
Ran initial tests to confirm shape matching for input arrays.

Closes #20

prs.title: Rename private helper functions to follow underscore convention
prs.body:
Closes #1011

This pull request updates internal helper functions and methods to follow the underscore (`_`) naming convention for private APIs used in the `icalendar` codebase.

Only functions and classes that are not exported via `icalendar.__all__` were modified. No public APIs were changed. All internal imports and references were updated accordingly, and existing tests were adjusted where needed.

The changes are intentionally scoped to keep the pull request small and easy to review, in line with the recommendations mentioned in the issue.

All tests were run locally and are passing. No documentation updates were required, as this change only affects internal implementation details.


<!-- readthedocs-preview icalendar start -->
----
📚 Documentation preview 📚: https://icalendar--1125.org.readthedocs.build/

<!-- readthedocs-preview icalendar end -->

prs.title: Test: Improve UI test coverage and fix Jest setup

labels.name: has merge conflicts
labels.description: PR has merge conflicts, require rebase

prs.body:
<!-- Please refer to CONTRIBUTING.md (https://github.com/fossology/FOSSologyUI/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md)
before creating the pull request to make sure you follow all the standards. -->

## Description

Add Jest configuration, Babel preset, and test-only mocks/shims so frontend UI tests run reliably in this repo.
Add beginner-friendly component tests and convert several fragile tests to React Testing Library where practical.
Keep production code unchanged; all edits are test-only (configs, mocks, and tests).

### Changes

• Added and updated test configuration:
  - .babelrc with preset-react (runtime: automatic)
  - Jest config and setup (jest.config.cjs, jest/setupTests.js)
  - Jest mocks/shims for react-dom, queryString, and file/style imports
  - UI stabilization via styledComponentsMock.js and jest/ui-mocks/*

• Added test coverage for:
  - index, footer, tree
  - Widget components (Alert, Button, Input, TextIcon)
  - UI inputs (input, textarea)

• Migrated index.test.jsx to React Testing Library (RTL)

## How to test

Install dependencies:
  yarn install

Run the test suite:
  yarn test --runInBand

Expected result:
  All frontend tests execute; on my run the suite passed (12 suites, 18 tests).

Quick checks:
  Confirm no production files were modified (only test/config files and new tests).
  Run single suites while iterating, for example:
    yarn test button.test.jsx


close #159

Perf: Optimized Next.js config for performance and security
prs.body:
Enhanced the project configuration for improved build efficiency, security, and scalability:

Added modern image formats (avif, webp) for faster load times

Removed console logs in production for cleaner builds

Added security headers (X-Frame-Options, Referrer Policy, etc.)

Enabled experimental features (scrollRestoration, serverActions)

Improved readability and maintainability with constants

prs.title: Fix: Resolved new user login issue in Pouranik
prs.body:
Resolved new user login issue in Pouranik
Enhanced user authentication flow by improving error handling and token generation logic. Fixed bug preventing new users from logging in immediately after registration. Added proper field validation, secure password hashing, and JWT implementation.

prs.title: Create counting_sort.cpp
prs.body:
This pull request adds a well-documented Counting Sort algorithm implementation in C++ to the repository as part of Hacktoberfest 2025. The code follows clean syntax, includes step-by-step comments, and demonstrates sorting an integer array efficiently using the Counting Sort technique.

Key Highlights:

Language: C++

Topic: Sorting Algorithm

Algorithm: Counting Sort

Includes clear comments for better understanding

Beginner-friendly and tested for correctness

issues.title: Feature: Add automated data loading and validation for taxon dataset
issues.body:
Currently, the project lacks a structured way to load and validate the mosquito taxon dataset. I propose adding a data_loader module that:

1. Loads features and labels from .tsv files.

2. Validates that the number of samples matches across files.

3. Includes basic unit tests for data integrity.
